description: Regex patterns for Nu HTML validator (vnu) error messages that are known, accepted artifacts and must not fail the build. Two categories: (1) KaTeX's MathML/HTML accessibility layer (inside `<span class="katex-mathml"><math>‚Ä¶`), whose element set and inline styles the validator rejects but which is required for screen-reader math support; and (2) CSS in the machine-generated, minified critical CSS that vnu's CSS checker cannot validate ‚ÄĒ modern features it predates (CSS Houdini `@property`, `var()`/`color-mix()` references it will not resolve) and a minified-value the penthouse extractor mis-serializes. Every entry is a Python `re` pattern matched against the vnu message text and must state why it is unfixable in source. Do NOT add site-authored HTML/CSS bugs here ‚ÄĒ fix those at the source (e.g. an invalid `line-height` value belongs in the SCSS, not this list).

allow.reason: KaTeX MathML elements; the validator does not enter MathML foreign-content mode for KaTeX's markup and reports its elements as unknown.

allow.pattern: Element ‚Äú(mrow|mo|mi|mn|ms|mtext|mspace|mpadded|mphantom|menclose|merror|maction|mfrac|msqrt|mroot|msub|msup|msubsup|munder|mover|munderover|mmultiscripts|mprescripts|mtable|mtr|mtd|mstyle|semantics|annotation)‚ÄĚ not allowed as child of element
allow.reason: KaTeX MathML child nesting the validator rejects once it has left MathML mode.

allow.pattern: not allowed as child of element ‚Äú(mrow|mo|mi|mn|ms|mtext|mfrac|msqrt|mroot|msub|msup|msubsup|munder|mover|munderover|mmultiscripts|mtable|mtr|mtd|mstyle|msubsup|semantics)‚ÄĚ
allow.reason: KaTeX MathML parent nesting the validator rejects once it has left MathML mode.
KaTeX MathML presentation attributes (e.g. empty `columnspacing`, `mathcolor`) that the validator flags but browsers render.
KaTeX `.vlist` vertical-alignment shims use negative heights in inline styles; this is core to KaTeX's HTML rendering.
CSS Houdini `@property` (registers the sidebar-fade lengths as typed custom properties in scroll-indicator.scss); a valid at-rule that vnu's CSS checker predates and does not recognize.

vnu's CSS checker cannot resolve `var()` references (the favicon `--attenuated-*` custom properties and `color-mix()` args), so it labels the property grammar `*invalid*`; the values are valid CSS that browsers resolve.

The penthouse critical-CSS extractor mis-serializes the minified reduced-motion reset (`animation-duration:.01ms`) to `NaNs`; the served full stylesheet keeps the correct value, so this is a transient extractor artifact, not an authored declaration.
